summary: The team is sprinting toward a mid-September launch of the joint CKGSB-IE report 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global' while simultaneously activating the Asia Start campaign and preparing the Intelligent China program for December.
summaryDetail: The single most important initiative is the joint report with IE Business School on Chinese companies going global, targeting a mid-September launch at Tech in Asia or the Tyson event in Singapore. This week, Felix must finalize the one-pager proposal and confirm the launch event, while the team begins translating the Chinese report and identifying alumni case studies. Simultaneously, the Asia Start campaign is launching with two parallel routes—AI-focused (Joseph) and traditional (Daisy)—for the first month, requiring immediate execution. For Intelligent China (December launch), Joseph needs to get the webpage ready, and Xuan must produce a one-pager plan for events in Beijing and Shanghai. Urgent this week: finalize the Shun Tian Shu press release (Julie), prioritize Media Minds for social media agency (Xuan), and send the CES press release to Professor Sun (Ira). Upcoming: decide on the Intelligent China campaign route and begin SCM campaign once the webpage is live.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting
meetings.summary: Team discussed website menu feedback, brochure updates, social media agency search, and IE report translation. Decisions made on Shun Tian Shu press release approach and prioritizing Media Minds for social media.

meetings.title: Joint Report on Chinese Companies Going Global
meetings.summary: Team discusses a joint report with CKGSB on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, to be launched at Tech in Asia or Tyson events in Singapore in September. They plan to include op-eds from deans and case studies, with a working title 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Discussed Asia Start campaign with two routes (AI vs traditional) and a new Intelligent China program launching in December. Need to start SCM campaign for the new program once the webpage is ready.

meetings.title: Europe-China Cooperation Discussion
meetings.summary: Discussion on geopolitical tensions affecting European companies in China, with emphasis on de-risking and the role of companies versus governments.

actionItems.detail: For the joint report.

actionItems.detail: To add industry perspective to the report.

actionItems.detail: To decide if the report can be launched there.

decisions.text: Only one Shun Tian Shu press release needed, either incorporating 10 points or turning into 10-point article.
Prioritize Media Minds for social media agency due to small budget and time constraints.
The report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE Business School.
decisions.meetingTitle: Joint Report on Chinese Companies Going Global

decisions.text: The working title is 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'.
decisions.meetingTitle: Joint Report on Chinese Companies Going Global

decisions.text: The report will include a main research feature by Professor Leeway, op-eds from deans, and industry case studies.
decisions.meetingTitle: Joint Report on Chinese Companies Going Global

decisions.text: Target launch in mid-September at either Tech in Asia or Tyson event in Singapore.
decisions.meetingTitle: Joint Report on Chinese Companies Going Global

decisions.text: Asia Start campaign will run two parallel routes for the first month: AI (Joseph) and traditional (Daisy).
Intelligent China program will target foreigners, with focus on module one in Beijing.
Agreed to use the first title option for the CES press release: 'CKGSB Professor Sun Tianshu at Summer Davos: AI’s Second Half Needs 1,000 Henry Fords'
decisions.meetingTitle: Re: CES press release - PR Newswire figures

decisions.text: Professor Li Wei agreed to collaborate with IE University on the joint report 'China's New Playbook for Going Global'
